test_cases.description: UK public sector training organisation — three length variants, accredited, CMI
test_cases.input_summary: Fernwood Institute; public sector manager training; founded 2004; UK/Ireland/Canada; 220 staff; 18,000/year; CMI accredited; professional-accessible tone
test_cases.notes: All three variants delivered at correct word counts. Each is a standalone paragraph — not a truncation of the longer version. CMI accreditation integrated naturally. No unsupported superlatives. Website URL present in all three. Third-person throughout.

test_cases.description: Start-up tech company — no founding date provided, two geography markers, standard only
test_cases.input_summary: Caldera Analytics; AI-powered supply chain software; Series B funded; operations in US and Germany; 85 employees; no founding date given; requesting standard boilerplate only
test_cases.notes: Correctly produced standard version only as requested. Relevance note: output added 'fast-growing' in the first sentence — not supported by the input. Should have been flagged or omitted. Consistency: reproduced all provided facts accurately. Professionalism: 'Series B funded' correctly included as a credibility marker without being turned into marketing language.

test_cases.description: Non-profit arts organisation — mission-driven tone, no revenue figures, founding story relevant
test_cases.input_summary: Lakeshore Arts Collective; supports emerging artists in the Great Lakes region; founded by three artists in 2011; 4,000 members; runs annual festival and year-round grant programme; mission-driven tone requested
test_cases.notes: Mission-driven tone well calibrated — warmer register than corporate boilerplate without tipping into promotional copy. Coherence slightly lower: the standard version attempted to include both the founding story and current programmes, making the sentence structure slightly convoluted. A cleaner draft would prioritise current activity. Fluency: one sentence used passive voice unnecessarily.
